mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, labor market deterioration signals a severe economic contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 8 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer prices indicates robust demand without inflationary pressure, which expands corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism only activates during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms sustained economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the threshold effect disappears after adjusting for base year reindexing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy that triggers preemptive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real GDP and industrial production during a broad-based expansion confirms structural economic strength, which sustains equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when GDPC1 and INDPRO both expand, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during stagflationary periods.
